outline.title: Christology
outline.summary: A systematic study of the person and work of Jesus Christ, examining biblical foundations, historical doctrine, and contemporary theological perspectives on Christ's incarnation, atonement, and significance.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Course Overview
outline.summary: Explore the central Christian claim that Jesus Christ is God incarnate, investigating how this claim has been understood throughout Christian history and how it applies to faith and practice today.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Learning Outcomes
outline.summary: Students will articulate biblical foundations for understanding Christ, trace historical christological development, analyze major theological models, and apply Christology to worship and mission.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Biblical Foundations
outline.summary: Examine messianic expectation in the Old Testament, Jesus' self-understanding, and apostolic proclamation of Christ in the New Testament as the foundation for Christian Christology.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Historical Development
outline.summary: Trace the development of christological doctrine from the early church councils (Nicaea, Chalcedon) through medieval, Reformation, and modern theological perspectives.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Contemporary Challenges
outline.summary: Engage contemporary theological movements including feminist Christology, process theology, pluralism, and Christology in non-Western contexts.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Pastoral Application
outline.summary: Apply christological understanding to Christian worship, preaching, pastoral counseling, ethics, and the church's mission in the world.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Christ at the Center
outline.summary: Christology is not merely academic study but the heart of Christian faith and practice. Understanding Christ shapes how we worship, serve, and witness.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed
